source_prompt: Design the execution workflow for Slice 0 of the canonical figure-agent spec (2026-06-21-iteration-first-figure-agent-spec.md §8). Method per §0: TDD + subagent-driven-development + clean-checkout proof. Entry B1, then B2/B4/B3. Slice-0 done = propose fig2_trap_design_space -> >=1 op -> candidate -> MCP apply -> compile fig2_trap_design_space --strict rc=0.

activation.reason: Multi-task slice with ordered handoffs (B2->B3->exit), per-task human review gates, and per-task adversarial verification (TDD failing-test + spec-compliance + code-quality review). Resumable by spec task IDs across /compact. Not a Workflow-runtime fan-out: tasks are dependency-coupled and human-gated, so execution is in-session subagent-driven-development, not the deterministic Workflow tool.

objective: Make the agent-driven element-iteration loop actually run-and-apply on a real figure: MCP apply wired to the working candidate_apply engine (B1), defect ledger fed by real render-detectors behind the adjudication gate (B2), candidate_families generalized off the hard-coded panel C (B3), critique routing decoupled from reference-gating (B4), semantic_assertions surfaced/generalized/gated (B5). Exit = the §7 Slice-0 exit probe passes on fig2 AND B1a passes on fig1, with full pytest green in a clean checkout.

surfaces.locator: examples/fig1_overview_v2_pair_001_vault (build/candidates/CAND001 exists on disk but is GITIGNORED/untracked)
examples/fig2_trap_design_space (UNTRACKED; owned by other work; carries working-tree VC010 change; DO NOT COMMIT)

assumptions.claim: fig1 has an applyable committed candidate for the B1a probe.
assumptions.verification: VERIFIED FALSE-ish: build/candidates/CAND001 exists on disk but is gitignored (git ls-files returns nothing) and CAND001/ has no acceptance.json (candidate_set.json lives at the candidates/ parent). The B1 unit test MUST self-construct its candidate fixture under tmp/ rather than depend on the gitignored CAND001, or it fails in a clean checkout.

assumptions.claim: propose fig2 yields >=1 op once candidate_families is generalized.
assumptions.verification: VERIFIED PARTIAL: propose (bin/fig-agent:539) goes ledger->quality_patch_plan, NOT candidate_families. candidate_families feeds the `candidates` command (candidate_generator.py:141). The exit chain spans BOTH: propose must yield ops (needs B2 ledger + B3 patch body in quality_patch_plan._default_patch) AND a candidate dir must be produced (needs B3 candidate_families generalized). B3 must touch both surfaces.

assumptions.claim: The RED test is a HEAD defect.
assumptions.verification: VERIFIED FALSE: test_real_fixture_audit_adoption is RED only because the untracked fig2-5 example dirs are on disk and _real_fixture_names() scans disk; the contract YAML lacks entries for them. In a clean checkout at HEAD (untracked dirs absent) it is likely GREEN. The RED is a working-tree artifact coupled to other-owned untracked dirs.

assumptions.claim: MCP can resolve candidate_set_path + acceptance_path from candidate_id.
assumptions.verification: PARTIAL: CLI requires --candidate-set and --acceptance (both required). candidate_apply defaults acceptance_path to build/candidates/{id}/acceptance.json (absent for CAND001). B1 must define canonical resolution AND ensure the acceptance gate actually runs (no bypass).

phases.title: Baseline + falsifiable probe harness
phases.exit: Two executable probe scripts (B1a, exit) exist and currently FAIL for the right reasons; baseline pytest count recorded; RED-test root cause documented.
tasks: record pytest baseline (working tree vs clean checkout)
author B1a probe script (fig1 MCP apply)
author exit probe script (fig2 propose->candidate->MCP apply->compile --strict)
snapshot fig2.tex sha for rollback discipline

phases.title: B1 - wire MCP apply -> candidate_apply
phases.exit: B1a probe passes on fig1 via MCP; acceptance/drift/lock gates run (not bypassed); unit test self-constructs its candidate fixture (clean-checkout safe).
tasks: TDD failing test: MCP figure_agent_apply_candidate mutates a .tex + recompiles clean

implement: replace refusal at 1368 with resolution of canonical candidate_set/acceptance paths + delegate to candidate_apply via the same CLI path the engine uses; extend inputSchema with candidate_set_path + acceptance_path
spec-compliance review
code-quality review

phases.title: B2 - defect ledger ingests render-detectors
phases.exit: An adjudicated fig2 visual_clash becomes a ledger defect carrying a concrete file:line.
tasks: TDD failing test: adjudicated fig2 visual_clash -> ledger defect with file:line
implement: ledger ingests visual_clash.json + undeclared_geometry.json (+ label_path_proximity) behind critique_adjudication gate, normalized via quality_patch_policy.classify_patchability
spec-compliance review
code-quality review

phases.title: B3 - generalize candidate_families + selector patch body (GATING)
phases.exit: propose fig2_trap_design_space yields >=1 operation AND a candidate dir is produced for fig2.
tasks: TDD failing test: propose fig2 -> >=1 operation

implement: derive SUPPORTED_PANEL/FAMILY/ENERGY_TERMS from spec.yaml panels[]+node text; replace _default_patch toy body with selector-based bounded-coordinate replace_text applicator; B3-handoff fallback (emit file:line + suggested 1-line patch instead of unsupported_defect)
spec-compliance review
code-quality review

phases.title: Slice-0 breadth - B4 + B5 + hygiene + RED-fix
phases.entry: P0 done (parallelizable; B5 gate decision required before landing)
phases.exit: B4: fig2 routes to critique. B5: semantic_assertions surfaced in status/next + authoring block templated + gate decided (BLOCKER vs surface-where-present). Hygiene: FP rate below stated threshold on a labeled TP/FP set. RED-fix: honest adoption_status decided per the untracked-ownership gate.
tasks: B4: add critique-required state keyed on audit_crops manifest OR unadjudicated_candidate_count; route next -> /fig_critique
B5: surface build/semantic_assertions.json in status/next; template authoring block; apply gate decision
hygiene: labeled TP/FP set -> demote bare undeclared_* to INFO -> retune _known_false_positives.yaml -> honest per-figure caps
RED-fix: per decision gate, add honest adoption_status entries or defer

phases.title: Slice-0 exit gate + clean-checkout proof
phases.exit: Exit probe passes on fig2 AND B1a passes on fig1 AND full pytest green in a fresh worktree clean checkout. Slice 1 unblocked.
tasks: run B1a probe (fig1)
run exit probe (fig2) with snapshot+rollback, never committing fig2
git worktree add --detach + uv sync + uv run pytest tests/ -q (expect 0 failed)
git worktree remove --force

workers.role: Fresh per-task implementer (TDD: makes the pre-written failing test pass with minimal change).

requires_escalation_for: touching files outside the task's named symbols

forbidden_actions: edit fig2-5 example .tex except via the candidate_apply rollback-safe probe in P5
weaken/skip a test to make it pass

must_include: the named target symbols from the spec §8 item

prompt_contract.required_output_schema: diff + test-run log showing the target test now passes and no previously-green test regressed
stop_conditions: target test passes and no regression
blocked by an undocumented hole -> stop and report
would need to touch a forbidden file -> stop and escalate

ownership: the single B-task's named symbols only

workers.role: Spec-compliance reviewer (does the change satisfy the §8 acceptance criterion and honor §6 gates without bypassing them?).
forbidden_actions: approve a change that bypasses the acceptance gate or fabricates success envelopes for no-ops (§6 honesty gate)

workers.role: Code-quality reviewer (style match, no over-engineering, no dead-weight, surgical edits per global CLAUDE.md).
forbidden_actions: demand refactors beyond the task scope

artifact_schema.validation_command: uv run python bin/fig-agent propose fig2_trap_design_space | jq '.. | objects | select(.file? and .line?)'

handoffs.artifact: propose fig2 yields >=1 operation + candidate dir

artifact_schema.validation_command: uv run python bin/fig-agent propose fig2_trap_design_space | jq '.operations | length >= 1'

parallelism.fan_in_rule: P5 is a barrier requiring BOTH P1 (B1) and P3 (B3, which depends on P2) complete. P1 runs in parallel with P2->P3. P4 breadth tasks parallelize on non-overlapping files but each passes the human review gate serially.

verification.claim_or_output: B1 MCP apply works without bypassing safety
verification.falsifier: B1a probe mutates fig1 .tex AND the acceptance/drift/lock gate is observably executed (not short-circuited); a clean-checkout unit test passes using a self-built candidate fixture
verification.evidence_required: probe exit=0 + diff in fig1*.tex + gate log + pytest line

verification.falsifier: an adjudicated fig2 visual_clash appears as a ledger defect with a concrete file:line
propose fig2_trap_design_space .operations length >= 1 (today []) AND candidate dir produced
the full exit chain: propose>=1op -> candidate -> MCP apply -> compile fig2_trap_design_space --strict rc=0
fresh worktree clean checkout: uv sync + uv run pytest tests/ -q == 0 failed

risk_gates.trigger: Exit probe mutates the UNTRACKED, other-owned fig2.tex (which carries an uncommitted VC010 change).
risk_gates.safe_default: Snapshot fig2.tex sha before; run apply; restore via candidate_apply rollback.patch (or git checkout is impossible since untracked, so use the file snapshot); NEVER commit fig2. Prefer running the exit probe against a copied sandbox of fig2 if feasible.

risk_gates.trigger: RED-fix would add adoption_status entries coupled to untracked, other-owned fig2-5 dirs.
risk_gates.safe_default: Do NOT commit YAML entries that reference untracked dirs; treat the RED as a working-tree artifact and verify the clean-checkout is green instead; defer real entries until the dirs' owner commits them.

risk_gates.trigger: B5 §6 gate: multi-panel figure entering acceptance without a semantic_assertions block = BLOCKER.
risk_gates.safe_default: Adopt the §9-risk-5 downgrade ('surface where present') for this slice to avoid blocking fig2's own exit probe; defer strict BLOCKER enforcement until fig2 has an authored block.

risk_gates.trigger: Any edit to dist/, examples/fig3_floating_clip_protocol, fig4_trap_energy_diagram, fig5_actuation_mechanism, or caption.md.
risk_gates.safe_default: Out of scope; forbidden; do not touch.
Branch is design/figure-agent-v2-svg; commit only on explicit user instruction, one logical change per B-task; never commit untracked other-owned fixtures.

resume.invalidation: spec §8 edit invalidates downstream task caches; a task is 'cached-done' only when its acceptance test is green at HEAD

first_slice.instruction: Execute B1: write the failing test first (MCP figure_agent_apply_candidate applies a candidate end-to-end on a self-built fixture, mutating a .tex and recompiling clean, with the acceptance/drift/lock gate observably running), then replace the hard refusal at mcp/figure_agent_server.py:1368 by resolving canonical candidate_set/acceptance paths and delegating to the same candidate_apply path the CLI uses, extending the tool inputSchema with candidate_set_path + acceptance_path. Verify via the B1a probe on fig1.
inputs: scripts/candidate_apply.py:341 + bin/fig-agent:876 as the reuse target
first_slice.expected_output: diff to mcp/figure_agent_server.py + a new clean-checkout-safe test; B1a probe exit=0 with fig1 .tex mutated and compile rc=0; acceptance gate log present
first_slice.completion_check: uv run pytest tests/ -q shows the new B1 test passing and no regression; B1a probe exit=0
forbidden_actions: depending on the gitignored build/candidates/CAND001 in the unit test
